LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO RESPONDENTS RE: ISSUANCE OF ALTERNATIVE WRIT ON MANDATE. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION FOR WRIT IS GRANTED. RESPONDENTS MUST FULLY COMPLY WITH THEIR STATUTORY DUTIES AS SPECIFIED IN W & I CODE SECTION 18914 AND 7 U.S.C. SECTION 2020. RESPONDENTS MUST SCREEN ALL EXPEDITED SERVICE APPLICATIONS ON A PRIORITY BASIS AT THE TIME AN APPLICANT INITIALLY SEEKS ASSISTANCE (W & I CODE SECTION 18914(B)) AND COMPLY WITH THE DEADLINES REGARDING THE RECEIPT OF EXPEDITED BENEFITS. WRIT DENIED AS TO ORAL NOTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS OF W & I CODE SECTION 18912 AS RESPONDENTS HAVE FULLY COMPLIED. RESPONDENTS MUST FILE A RETURN TO THIS WRIT ON OR BEFORE JUNE 30, 2010 SETTING FORTH WHAT THEY HAVE DONE TO COMPLY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Petitioner'S Order To Show Cause To Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10)
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO RESPONDENTS RE: ISSUANCE OF ALTERNATIVE WRIT ON MANDATE. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition for writ is granted. Respondents must fully comply with their statutory duties as specified in W & I Code section 18914 and 7 U.S.C. section 2020. Respondents must screen all expedited service applications on a priority basis at the time an applicant initially seeks assistance (W & I Code section 18914(b)) and comply with the deadlines regarding the receipt of expedited benefits. Writ denied as to oral notification requirements of W & I Code section 18912 as respondents have fully complied. Respondents must file a return to this writ on or before June 30, 2010 setting forth what they have done to comply. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797

Petitioner'S Order To Show Cause To Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10)
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner's Order to Show Cause to Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10). No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Order to show cause is continued to May 18, 2010 for parties to submit evidence regarding compliance or noncompliance with 7 U.S.C. section 2020, and Cal. W & I Code sections 18914 and 18912. Evidence to be submitted simultaneously on or before May 4, 2010. Simultaneous reply evidence to be submitted on or before May 11, 2010. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Petitioner'S Order To Show Cause To Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10)
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Continued To Apr-14-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner's Order to Show Cause to Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10). No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Hearing is continued to April 14, 2010 to determine whether respondent has achieved compliance. (See Morris vs. CYA (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 52, 59.) Parties to provide joint status report on April 8, 2010 regarding compliance. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
